commit.message:
feat: automated unit handling for recipes

Auto-create standard recipe units in Grocy (g, kg, dl, l, ml, tl, rkl,
rs, kpl) with global conversions on first recipe scrape. Recipe
positions now use the correct quantity unit instead of always using the
product's stock unit (Piece).

AI-powered package size detection analyses product names via Gemini to
create product-specific conversions (e.g. 'Maito 1L' → 1 piece = 1 l).
Stock comparison uses these conversions for accurate green/yellow/red
status. Shopping list calculates purchase amounts in pieces.

Frontend shows amount + unit next to ingredient names (e.g. '8 dl')
with colour-only stock status instead of meaningless piece counts.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- .gitignore-aware directory filtering during scan
- Type-aware branch creation tasks (git -C vs git submodule update)
- Configuration via .specify/init-options.json (optional)
- Full core plan/tasks workflow preserved with additive changes only

commit.message:
fix: close autonomous review loop for agentic PRs

Root cause: agentic workflow PRs were auto-expiring after 2-3 days
because the review loop was broken:
- pr-docs-reviewer skipped agentic PRs (skip-bots: [github-actions])
- pr-docs-reviewer could only comment, not approve/reject
- No approval → auto-merge never triggered → PRs expired

Changes:
- Upgrade pr-docs-reviewer to autonomous reviewer:
  - Remove github-actions from skip-bots
  - Add submit-pull-request-review (APPROVE/REQUEST_CHANGES)
  - Add create-pull-request-review-comment for inline feedback
  - Update prompt: approve on pass, request changes on 🔴 issues
- Enable auto-merge + draft:false on 4 workflows missing it:
  docs-vnext-sync, glossary-maintainer, slide-deck-maintainer,
  label-ops-docs-fix
- Extend PR expiry from 2d/3d to 7d across all 9 PR-creating
  workflows as safety net
- Remove discussion-responder workflow (GitHub Discussions not
  enabled on this repo)

Expected flow: workflow creates PR → pr-docs-reviewer reviews →
APPROVE → auto-merge completes

fix(nu): gracefully skip init when oh-my-posh is not installed (#7442)

* fix(nu): add early return guard when oh-my-posh is not available

If a user uninstalls oh-my-posh without removing the autoload script,
the script would still run on every Nu startup and produce errors.

Add an early return guard at the top of omp.nu that exits the script
immediately if the executable is no longer available. Handles both
install modes:
- Absolute path (standard install): \path exists\
- Base name only (MSIX / --strict mode): \which\

Fixes #7245

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(msi): accept WiX v7 EULA in build script

WiX v7 now requires explicit EULA acceptance under the Open Source
Maintenance Fee policy. Add -acceptEula wix7 to the wix build command
so CI releases don't fail.

See: https://docs.firegiant.com/wix/osmf/#direct-acceptance

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(nu): guard path exists check to absolute/relative paths only

When the executable is stored as a bare name (e.g. MSIX / --strict mode),
\path exists\ resolves against the current working directory and may
incorrectly return true if an unrelated file with the same name exists
there. Only apply the \path exists\ check when the executable string
looks like a path (contains a separator or starts with . / ~); otherwise
rely solely on \which\ for the PATH-based lookup.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix dynamic Function new.target leak and generator callable dispatch

- Add push_dynamic_function_call_new_target() helper to isolate
  new.target for BUILTIN_CTOR_FUNCTION/BUILTIN_NEW_FUNCTION calls,
  preventing outer constructor new.target from leaking into
  CreateDynamicFunction and corrupting the prototype chain.

- Fix __fn_body__ callable_expr to use function*/async function/
  async function* for generator/async dynamic functions.

- Split eval+call for generators: eval the function expression first,
  then call it separately so the generator's bytecode outlives the
  eval scope (fixes generators returning {done:true} immediately).

- Route all dynamic generator calls in runner.rs through
  vm_call_function_value instead of manual yield parsing, fixing
  return value handling in dynamic generators.

Cluster 32: 581→595 pass (+14), Cluster 29: 701→704 pass (+3)

* Skip secondary vtable emission on COFF path to fix LNK2005 duplicate RTTI symbols

Secondary vtables (with non-zero offset_to_top) are an Itanium/ELF concept.
On the COFF/MSVC path, emitting them causes duplicate RTTI symbols like
??_R1.?AVDerived@@8 because ObjFileWriter::add_vtable unconditionally emits
RTTI structures keyed by class_name without deduplication.

* Fix use-after-move: defer vtable_info move until after secondary vtable registration

* Guard secondary vptr stores with Itanium ABI check to fix LNK2019 on COFF

The emitVptrStores lambda was emitting MemberStore ops referencing
$flash$vtable$ secondary vtable symbols on both ELF and COFF paths.
These symbols are only generated by the ELF vtable emission code,
causing LNK2019 unresolved externals on Windows/COFF.

* Fix COFF virtual base construction

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Simplify collectReachableVBases: avoid redundant traversal of virtual_bases and base_classes

Since StructTypeInfo::virtual_bases already contains all transitively
reachable virtual bases, we only need to iterate virtual_bases for
collection and recurse through non-virtual bases in base_classes.
Remove the redundant virtual-base collection from base_classes and
the unnecessary recursion into virtual bases (which are shared and
already fully captured by the virtual_bases list).

* Fix use-after-move: save ctor_decl_op.mangled_name before std::move

* Add TODO comment documenting missing destructor thunks in secondary vtables

* Add known issue: remove default ConstructorVariant parameters in NameMangling.h
